channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Cellular wrap: existing within each other
item.description: Wednesday / June 10, 2026 at 2:30 pm This 75 minute session is dedicated to a movement inquiry and experiment through improvised gestures and the sounding body. Using a practice of embodied research that borrows from cellular science, participants are invited to explore senses of closeness between human bodies and land ecologies. What lives within each of them? What does my body sense when moving closely with, not solely across, the land I occupy? How might moving with closeness as a collective…
item.author: Community

item.title: Shows Announced! TOPOS 2026 Music Festival | September 2–5
item.description: Date: Monday, June 8, 2026 (all day) - Every TOPOS 2026 presentation is a way into our own stories, and of those around us. Featuring Vijay Iyer, Wadada Leo Smith, Miho Hatori, Edward George, Paul Abbott, IMA, Rock City Falls Trio (Improv Spaces), Sun Dogs (Improv Spaces), Fred Moten, and The Silent Light—TOPOS 2026 brings together different forms of music, not as genres but as provocative sites of experience. A free BBQ at EMPAC keeps the conversation going! Find the full festival schedule,…
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Seminar: “Supervisory Essentials” July 22, 2026
item.description: In this seminar, Managers and Supervisors will learn from each other and our Facilitators about important topics such as the Employee Value Proposition and Employee Experience, the six fundamental Leadership competencies at Rensselaer, and experience a “day in the life” of a Supervisor. In addition, the importance of managing employee performance, providing both rewards and recognition, employment law fundamentals and tools for building team capacity will be explored. For more information…
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: RPI at Work MBA for Professionals Review
item.description: Date: Wednesday, June 10, 2026 12:00 PM - 12:30 PM - Learn to lead from every aspect of an organization with a real-world MBA experience. Prepare to lead the most dynamic and innovative organizations. Engage, involve, motivate, and lead others to reach their fullest potential. Join RPI Dean Aric Krause, Ph. D., as he reviews this innovative project-based, online MBA for professionals program. Please register to receive login details to join this virtual event.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Leadercamp: “Balancing Work and Life in an Always-On World” June 16, 2026 - 9AM-10AM or 12PM-1PM
item.description: In today’s always-on world, there is an expectation of constant availability, leaving little room for rest, recovery, or even a moment to think. Over time, that relentless pressure takes a real toll, burnout is often the result. Boundary expert Terri Cole explains the importance of boundaries and how to identify where they are needed, with practical strategies for protecting your energy & building a more sustainable way of working & living without sacrificing your leadership or…
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Office of Strategic Alliances and Translation (OSAT) - Office Hours
item.description:
Date: Thursday, June 11, 2026 11:15 AM - 12:30 PM - The Office of Strategic Alliances and Translation invites you to in-person office hours on Thursday, June 11, 2026, 11:15 AM – 12:15 PM. Join us if you have questions about creating, licensing, or monetizing IP; determining if your idea has commercial potential; creating a startup or new venture; or working with industry for research or partnerships. Open to all RPI faculty and staff. No RSVP required.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
